blocks.content: The room where Shirone's group had stayed was neatly organized.
A black sphere was born in a space filled with still atmosphere.
The sphere looked like a two-dimensional hole, and five people were expelled from it.
Amy, who had rolled onto the floor, crawled toward the portal, calling for Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: She couldn't believe he wasn't coming back.
Tess and Arin grabbed her as she tried to go back into the portal.
Amy resented the two for stopping her.
She felt they shouldn't have left Shirone behind.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Let go! Everyone get out of the way! I'm going to save Shirone!

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Amy! Once you go in, you can't come back! We have to wait now!

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: What are we waiting for? You left Shirone behind in danger!

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: No! Peope…!
Arin stopped mid-sentence and looked at the portal.
From the other side, Shirone was spinning and flying towards them.
Tears welled up in Amy's eyes.
She caught Shirone in her arms and rolled across the floor under the power of the spiral.
She was so happy she couldn't feel any pain.
Arin watched the Meta Gate portal disappear and thought of the one fairy left in Heaven.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Thank you, Peope.
The portal had opened in the room provided by Chief Kerugo.
Heaven is a long way away, but since they've been through Gorphin's Gate, there's a lack time relativity.
Calculating the difference in solar calendars, it seems like about three days have passed.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Rian! Rian….
Tess's tears fell onto Rian's face.
Though he had returned alive, she wasn't happy.
He had always said he would become the greatest swordsman in the world, but now he had lost his arm.
Rian's right arm was reduced to nothing but bones.
They had tried to remove the greatsword, but his fingers wouldn't open.
Tess was at a loss.
How could he hold a sword when he had no muscle and his ligaments were torn and dangling?
At that moment, the door burst open, and a man of Kerugo entered.
He stared blankly at the women who were hugging their partners and giving them wary looks, then ran toward the exit and shouted.

blocks.speaker: Kerugo
blocks.content: They're back! They've returned!
Arin read the hostility in the man's eyes.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Arin, what's going on?

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: I don't know. But I think we should leave quickly. There's definitely no friendly feeling.
Amy's group, carrying the wounded, hurried out of the room.
The cave structure was complex, but no one had forgotten the way out.
The problem was that it was a one-way path.
Footsteps were heard from the other side of the cave, and dozens of warriors blocked the exit.

blocks.speaker: Kerugo
blocks.content: Where do you think you're going, you traitors!
Arin interpreted language through telepathy.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: The sin of insulting Kerugo cannot be repaid even with death! For three days and nights, we will flay your flesh and dry you under the sun until you die!
The language, even though translated, sent chills down their spines.
Amy couldn't understand the situation.
They hadn't fulfilled Kadum's request, but it wasn't a contract from the beginning.
Among the warriors, they saw an old man with familiar features.
His face was deeply wrinkled, and although he had lost some muscle, his figure still held some of its former bulk.

blocks.speaker: Kadum
blocks.content: You… No way?

blocks.speaker: Kadum
blocks.content: These damn things!
It was the voice of Chief Kerugo Kadum.
Is it possible to age so much in just a few days? Perhaps decades had passed while they were in heaven.

blocks.speaker: Kadum
blocks.content: How dare you try to destroy Kerugo! Give me back my lifespan!

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: We don't know anything about it! Why have you become like this?

blocks.speaker: Kadum
blocks.content: Nonsense! If it weren't for your tricks, why else would my lifespan have shortened? I could have lived another 60 years!
Arin suddenly realized.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Canis….
Even if all the men had a chance to manipulate the lifespan record, Canis was the only one who would make such a choice.
She supported his decision.
Considering what Kadum had done to the tribespeople, it was actually lenient.
But even Canis probably didn't know that things would turn out like this.
As the warriors approached with a powerful aura, Amy instinctively entered Spirit Zone.
She looked for Mahatu but couldn't see him.
If the Anti-Angel faction had been purged, this place was now the heart of enemy territory. There was no one left to help.

blocks.speaker: Kerugo
blocks.content: Capture them alive! I want to give these wretches a horrific death!
The Kerugo warriors advanced with their weapons.
The individual strength was impressive, but the collective fighting spirit was suffocating.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: What should I do? Should I block the path with Fire Wall? But it's dangerous in a cave.
Just as five burly warriors readied their spears and charged, a commotion was heard from the exit.
A terrified Kadum turned around and shouted.

blocks.speaker: Kadum
blocks.content: What's going on? What's happening!
As his body aged, his mind seemed to have deteriorated as well.
Soon, the warriors guarding the rear were thrown towards Amy's group.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: You good?
Amy's face went blank.
Mahatu, who she thought had been captured, was standing there.
Even more surprising were the people he had brought with him.
After finishing the interpretation and parting ways, Jis was standing under the escort of the Parrot Mercenary, and even the leader Marsha and vice-leader Freeman were there.

blocks.speaker: Jis
blocks.content: Jis? What are you doing here?

blocks.speaker: Jis
blocks.content: No time to explain! Quickly, come this way!
Before Jis could finish speaking, the Kerugo warriors charged again.

blocks.speaker: Kerugo
blocks.content: From their point of view, they absolutely had to capture Shirone's group.
The Kerugo Autonomous Region was already in the midst of a rebellion.
The residents joined the Anti-Angel faction, and if things settled down like this, the Angel faction wouldn't be able to avoid collapse.

blocks.speaker: Marsha
blocks.content: Freeman! Go get the kids!
Marsha kicked Freeman's butt with her foot.
Freeman, who hadn't taken the Reverse Technique Tree, showcased his swift movements.
The warriors' spears could not cut off even a single hair on his head.
It was the Gunner's Schema, prioritizing speed over regenerative power.
Thwack! Thwack! Thwack!
Freeman, who had entered the e heart of the enemy camp, fired his guns in all directions, causing the warriors to slam into the cave walls.
Soon, only Freeman remained, surrounded by thick smoke from the magic bullets.
Amy couldn't believe this was the same man she had faced before.
Changing his Schema had completely altered his combat style.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Let's go. We can't hold them off for long.

blocks.speaker: Jis
blocks.content: Ah, yes!
Amy's group followed the Parrot Mercenaries and left the area.
Kadum's warriors pursued them, but their numbers were no longer threatening.

blocks.speaker: Kadum
blocks.content: No! Don't go! I don't want to die like this! Please, give me back my lifespan!
Kadum's desperate cries echoed through the cave.
While leaving the Toa Mountains, Shirone's group listened to Mahatu explain the situation.
As Kadum's body aged, the priests declared martial law and imprisoned the Anti-Angel faction.
The person who rescued them was Jis.
Having arrived in the autonomous region to settle Falcoa's debt, he sensed the uneasy atmosphere and started investigating.
Then, realizing that Mahatu was locked up in prison, he arrived here leading Parrot Mercenaries.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Thank you, Jis. We wouldn't have survived without you.
Jis's face turned red at Amy's compliment.

blocks.speaker: Jis
blocks.content: This is nothing compared to what you guys did for me. But are you all right? Why are only the men in such a state?
Amy checked Shirone's condition.
There was still no sign of him waking up.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: It just happened that way. Can we find a healer on the island, or should we head to the Magic Guild?
Marsha looked at the injured.
Even though Shirone and Canis were just unconscious, Rian's condition was critical.
The flesh on his right arm was completely torn off.
With a wound of this severity, he should have died from excessive bleeding, but for some reason, no blood flowed.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Why does Rian look like this? No, more than that, how did you deal with him? Is he really okay like this?

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: I don't know. He ate the giant's arm, and it seems to have something to do with it. As long as As long as he's alive, we'll find a way to heal him.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: A giant? Ate an arm? What are you talking about?
Tess could only shake her head.
As they reached the outskirts of the Toa Mountains, the Parrot Mercenaries' carriage awaited them.
Mahatu bowed his head and expressed his guilt.

blocks.speaker: Mahatu
blocks.content: The immorality of our Chief has put the messenger of God in danger. Please forgive me.
The Anti-Angel faction was formed to oppose those who used the name of God to satisfy their own desires.
From Heaven's perspective, it was heresy and a way of thinking that could trigger the final war, but Amy couldn't blame Mahatu.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: I guess some rascal among our friend was displeased with Kadum. We are sorry instead. It's fortunate that things turned out well, but it could have cost many lives.

blocks.speaker: Mahatu
blocks.content: It was something that had to happen. If Kadum had been strong, overthrowing the regime would have required far more sacrifices. Thank you.
Amy waited for Mahatu to say something else.
But he didn't ask anything to the person who had been to heaven.
He chose independence.
Amy boarded the carriage.
Just as Mahatu's priority was the well-being of his people, so was her friend's well-being.
Mahatu did not move until Amy's group disappeared beyond the horizon.
Marsha had found one physician and four healing mages from Galliant Island and brought them to Jis's house.
The physician, seeing Rian's condition, was horrified.
He prayed that the boy had not been conscious while undergoing such torture.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Thi- This is...
Tess clung to him, crying.

blocks.speaker: Physician
blocks.content: Physician-nim, please heal him! You can, right?
The physician was silent as if mute.
What on earth did she want him to do? Healing meant restoring something that was broken.
He couldn't create something new where there was nothing.

blocks.speaker: Physician
blocks.content: There's nothing I can do. I'm not an excellent physician, but neither is anyone else. If we don't amputate, he might die.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Amputate? This kid is a swordsman! He's determined to become the world's strongest swordsman!

blocks.speaker: Physician
blocks.content: You'll have to cut off everything below the shoulder if he doesn't want to become the world's greatest corpse.
Tess fell to her knees weakly.
The physician was right.
Seeing the skeletal arm upon regaining consciousness would drive anyone mad.
The bleeding had stopped, but it could start again at any moment.

blocks.speaker: Physician
blocks.content: Ugh! This is… not a matter to tease.
The healing mages paired up to cast healing spells on Shirone and Canis.
They were using healing to enhance biological functions and purification to clear the mind, but it felt like pouring water into a bottomless pit.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: What on earth happened? Who are these children? I've never treated someone for this long and not seen them wake up.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: They're students of the Magic Academy. It'll be harder for them than for others.

blocks.speaker: Physician
blocks.content: Ugh! If we keep this up for more than ten minutes, we'll need an hour's rest. We're already beyond our limit!

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Please, just hold on for ten more minutes! We'll pay you whatever you want.

blocks.speaker: Physician
blocks.content: At this age, I have money. How about a date instead? You look just like my first love.
Amy opened her eyes wide and quickly nodded.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Yes? Okay, I'll do it. Just please keep trying.

blocks.speaker: Physician
blocks.content: …Don't make an old man's wit sound dirty. Anyway, they must be important to you. I'll do what I can.

blocks.speaker: Physician
blocks.content: Thank you! Thank you so much!
As Amy repeatedly expressed her gratitude, the mage casting Purify clicked his tongue and scolded his colleague.

blocks.speaker: Mage
blocks.content: You, you shouldn't make such harsh jokes.

blocks.speaker: Mage
blocks.content: Who knew it would turn out like this?

blocks.speaker: Mage
blocks.content: No, I do remember clearly that your first love was the ugliest of the ugly.
The two old men burst into laughter.
They were unofficial 9th-rank mage who had never seen the light of day in their entire lives, but for the first time in a long time, their blood was boiling.

blocks.speaker: Mage
blocks.content: Shall we go full power for the first time in a while?
As they amplified their healing magic, Shirone's body began to glow brightly.
The mages on Canis' side, who had developed a competitive spirit, also strengthened their magic, not wanting to be outdone.
The physician, ready for surgery, instructed Tess.

blocks.speaker: Physician
blocks.content: Allright, lay him down over here. There will be a lot of blood, so prepare plenty of towels.
Tess moved Rian's body to the center of the bed, her eyes watering.
The physician then pulled out a large, menacing saw from his bag.
With Rian unconscious, there was no need for anesthesia.
Even if he were awake, the pain would be unbearable with just local anesthesia.
